channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-26
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. The **docpluck** project has made significant strides with the addition of a new feature for table detection and updates to its documentation, laying the groundwork for future developments. Meanwhile, **escicheck** has been synchronized with the **escicheckapp** repository, bringing it in line with the latest version of **effectcheck**. Additionally, **auto-zcurve** has improved its validation note with a revised note and added citation, while **output-it-forward** has enhanced user accessibility by adding a Chrome Webstore link. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, and we celebrate the hard work and dedication that has gone into these projects. As our community continues to grow and evolve, we look forward to seeing the impact of these advancements.

44 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck, auto-zcurve

Most active:

docpluck16 commits
regcheck12 commits
metacheck10 commits
coarse3 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: metacheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with two specific changes made to the repository. The home page text was updated to point to the Metacheck Manual, as stated in the commit message "Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual". Additionally, the navigation bar was modified to replace the "Get Started/Reference/Articles" links with a single link to the Metacheck Manual, as per the commit message "Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link". The changes were made by Lakens on June 25, 2026. No other branches were worked on during this time.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 32659a9 [main] Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual — Lakens
  • d6a7f12 [main] Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link — Lakens
  • b8ac461 [dev] Fix coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure — Lakens
  • de41849 [dev] Add R/import-papers.R so dev installs cleanly — Lakens
  • ed8c8d3 [dev] Merge pull request #317 from scienceverse/papers_databases — Lisa DeBruine
  • e077134 [dev] Merge pull request #316 from scienceverse/ethics_check — Lisa DeBruine
  • a40d7db [dev] Merge pull request #309 from scienceverse/prereg_regcheck — Lisa DeBruine
  • 1fb2bab [dev] Merge pull request #318 from scienceverse/fix/code-check-paperlist — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8cbef44 [dev] Fix code_check crash and missing paper_id on paper lists — Lakens
  • b7afa6a [dev] add functions to load paper databases from scienceverse/papers — Lakens
  • 25c15a6 [main] update metacheck page to fix #314 — Lakens
  • 73736b0 [dev] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [dev] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens
  • dccb9f4 [main] update module names in vignettes — Lakens
  • 684ccdf [main] Update DESCRIPTION fix #313 — Daniel Lakens
  • 7d7127d [new_modules] fix #313 — Lakens
  • 9749095 [new_modules] explore ideas for z-curve and grim — Lakens
  • a7dea29 [main] Merge pull request #312 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where a feature was added for "RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables" as part of version 2.4.98, laying the foundation. The documentation was also updated on the 'main' branch with a partial addition of "2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons". Additionally, on the 'main' branch, a fix was made to the tooling, which included adding a "direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note" and incorporating "RC-T/RC-1 spec findings". The 'main' branch was the sole focus of these changes, with no other branches mentioned. The commits suggest a focus on table detection, documentation, and tooling fixes.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 521089a [main] feat(tables): RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables (v2.4.98, foundation) — Gilad Feldman
  • 976af30 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons (PARTIAL) — Gilad Feldman
  • 59cff5b [main] fix(tooling): direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note + RC-T/RC-1 spec findings — Gilad Feldman
  • 3e9cc2c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(learnings): RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97) — Gilad Feldman
  • 813aa4c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(tables): v2.4.97 — DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress — Gilad Feldman
  • 84a4d42 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96) — Gilad Feldman
  • 7247285 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(handoff+todo): RC-T implementation handoff + log RC-T deferral / close collabra.77859 adjudication — Gilad Feldman
  • 927d869 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(spec): RC-T table-region prose-contamination — confirmed 2-layer root cause + fix plan — Gilad Feldman
  • db7192b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(triage+learnings): v2.4.95 corpus assessment — 7/7 canary FAIL, RC-B7 already done (W0h), RC-T root-caused — Gilad Feldman
  • 2dbdd98 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate) — Gilad Feldman
  • c1fa68b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(todo): log v2.4.95 / REQUEST_11 shipped + deferred follow-ups (effect_type opt-in, 77859 caption-number, text-layer caveat) — Gilad Feldman
  • 370b89c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(handoff): record the cross-session skill-file commit coordination (now fulfilled in b2c6aa2) — Gilad Feldman
  • b2c6aa2 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] chore(docs+deploy): prior-session housekeeping — REQUEST_10 customer broadcast, daily-digest cron-retry lesson, deploy smoke-gate fix — Gilad Feldman
  • bce4b7f [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(tables): REQUEST_11 — flatten fields for non-clinical result tables (v2.4.95) — Gilad Feldman
  • 83ab6bf [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push — Gilad Feldman
  • 1158b1b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94) — Gilad Feldman
  • 2fd8711 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary) — Gilad Feldman
  • c671929 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal — Gilad Feldman
  • cf420ca [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92) — Gilad Feldman
  • f392957 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(iterate): v2.4.91 shipped handoff + cycle journal — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of recent work, where the multistudy ingestion was improved. On the staging branch, a debug of the CLI report was performed by Jamie Cummins. Additionally, on the staging branch, further local LLM support was added in the CLI. The staging branch also received UI fixes, as part of the recent changes made by Jamie Cummins.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 31fa103 [staging] improve multistudy ingestion — Jamie Cummins
  • b0d8ab2 [staging] debug of CLI report — Jamie Cummins
  • 97e8203 [staging] add further local LLM support in CLI — Jamie Cummins
  • df873ec [staging] UI fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • 5b53ad6 [staging] fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins
  • fbd5030 [staging] firm up qwen formatting — Jamie Cummins
  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins
  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins
  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that synchronized it with the `escicheckapp` repository at version `f37b5a1`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.6`, as stated in the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6)". This commit is associated with issue `#20`. No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The commit brought the `main` branch in line with the specified version of `effectcheck`. The `main` branch now reflects the changes present in `escicheckapp` at `f37b5a1`.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman
  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: auto-zcurve: activity update
item.description:
shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve

The `main` branch was worked on, where the validation note was revised and a citation was added, as stated in the commit message "Revise validation note and include citation". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The revision of the validation note and the inclusion of a citation are the specific changes made in this commit. The commit message does not mention any other changes or additions to the repository. Only one commit was made, which was to the `main` branch, and it involved revising and adding a citation to the validation note.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
